tossups.question: Poetry accompanied pictures by this artist in an April 2020 online MoMA ("moh-mah") exhibition titled for this artist's "Words and Pictures". This artist took pictures of the hands and feet of Indonesian dancers while accompanying Paul Taylor on a trip to Asia. A photograph by this artist of young men on a porch was transformed into a propaganda poster reading "This is your America... Keep it free!" This artist took pictures of (*) empty roads for the collection An American Exodus. Photographs of a man hoisting his grandson on his back and of an American flag flying in front of a barracks were taken by this artist for the War Relocation Board. Another of her photographs shows two children leaning on the shoulders of Florence Owens Thompson. For 10 points, name this photographer of Migrant Mother.
tossups.question_sanitized: Poetry accompanied pictures by this artist in an April 2020 online MoMA ("moh-mah") exhibition titled for this artist's "Words and Pictures". tossups.question: A late-career series by this photographer captured the work of Public Defenders in California. This photographer retouched one work in order to remove the subject’s thumb from the lower-right corner. This photographer collaborated with Pirkle Jones to document the deliberate destruction of Monticello in Death of a Valley. In one of this artist’s photographs, a man in a beat-up hat looks away from a crowd while clasping his hands. Unlike (*) Ansel Adams, this photographer was able to document early and late stages of internment at Manzanar. While working for the FSA, this artist of The White Angel Breadline photographed two children sheltering behind Florence Owens Thompson. For 10 points, name this woman who took the photograph Migrant Mother.

While working for the FSA, this artist of The White Angel Breadline photographed two children sheltering behind Florence Owens Thompson. For 10 points, name this woman who took the photograph Migrant Mother.

tossups.question: A documentary references this artist's desire to go down to the street and “grab a hunk of lightning”. This person collaborated with economist Paul Schuster Taylor on a project titled An American Exodus. This artist created a work of art depicting a man in a hat with a cup in San Francisco looking away from a line at a location run by the “White Angel”. Along with various male artists, she documented life at the (*) Manzanar camp for the interned Japanese during World War II. This artist's most famous image is of a pensive looking Florence Owens Thompson and her children at a pea pickers' camp in California during the Great Depression. For 10 points, name this photographer for the Farm Security Administration, best known for her picture Migrant Mother.

Along with various male artists, she documented life at the (*) Manzanar camp for the interned Japanese during World War II. This artist's most famous image is of a pensive looking Florence Owens Thompson and her children at a pea pickers' camp in California during the Great Depression. For 10 points, name this photographer for the Farm Security Administration, best known for her picture Migrant Mother.

tossups.question: This artist showed three men from Vermont leaning on a car parked next to a tent in a work captioned "Little Money." This artist collaborated with Pirkle Jones in depicting the desolation of Monticello, California before it was submerged under Lake Beryessa. That collaboration was published as the "Death of a Valley" issue of Aperture magazine. A picture taken by this artist shows Japanese children saying the (*) pledge of allegiance, and she also documented the Manzanar Japanese internment camp. While working for the Farm Safety Administration, she photographed Florence Owens Thomson and her two children at a campsite during the Great Depression. For 10 points, name this American photographer who took Migrant Mother.

While working for the Farm Safety Administration, she photographed Florence Owens Thomson and her two children at a campsite during the Great Depression. For 10 points, name this American photographer who took Migrant Mother.

bonuses.leadin: Three children grasp at a barbed wire fence in a photograph taken in this location. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this location. Mount Williamson can be seen in the background of several photographs of this location published in the book Born Free and Equal.

This photographer collaborated with Ansel Adams to compile photographs into Two Views of Manzanar. Ralph Merritt only allowed this man’s photographs to be physically taken by a white person, due to him being a prisoner at Manzanar himself.

This photographer captured sites like the Children’s Village of Manzanar before the WRA impounded over eight hundred of her photographs. This photographer depicted Florence Owens Thompson gazing into the distance in her Migrant Mother.

parts: Name this National Park in the Sierra Nevada where Ansel Adams took photos such as El Capitan and Moon over Half Dome.

In 1942, this photographer also took photos of Mount Williamson overlooking Manzanar. This photographer, who was later employed by the FSA, documented the Depression era in images such as White Angel Breadline.

bonuses.leadin: Many important moments in history have been cataloged by photographers. For 10 points each:
parts: This early photographer, known as the “father of photojournalism,” captured many scenes of the Civil War, some of which he depicted in his “Dead of Antietam” Exhibition. He also took the photo of Abraham Lincoln that is now featured on the penny.

This California photographer chronicled events at a Japanese Internment camp called Manzanar and also captured a famous image of the family of Florence Owens Thompson during the Great Depression in her “Migrant Mother.”
In June of 1963, this man captured a photo of Thich Quang Duc immolating himself on a Saigon street. That image won the Pulitzer Prize and quickly became known as “The Burning Monk.”

This California photographer chronicled events at a Japanese Internment camp called Manzanar and also captured a famous image of the family of Florence Owens Thompson during the Great Depression in her "Migrant Mother."
In June of 1963, this man captured a photo of Thich Quang Duc immolating himself on a Saigon street. That image won the Pulitzer Prize and quickly became known as "The Burning Monk."

bonuses.leadin: During this time period, Walker Evans photographed a portrait of Allie Mae Burroughs titled Alabama Tenant Farmer Wife. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this period of history. During this period, the artist of White Angel Breadline took several photographs of Florence Owens Thompson with her family.
While working for the Farm Security Administration, this female artist took the photograph Migrant Mother.

Photographer Lewis Hine, who worked for the TVA during the Depression, gained fame for his many photographs of these types of people, including one photo showing four of them side by side after leaving a mine.
answers: Great Depression [or the Great Slump; or the Depression; or the Dust Bowl; prompt on the 1930s]
child laborers [or synonymous answers, such as child workers; prompt on children and other synonyms]

bonuses.leadin: James Franco tried to reshoot this series with himself in the main role, but god it was awful. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this series of black-and-white, mock-vintage photographs. Its images include the photographer looking out skeptically at a cityscape and standing against an old door. It shows the photographer in a variety of stereotypical female roles like housewife and career girl.

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, answer the following about the photography of Life magazine:
parts: The first Life cover image was produced by this photographer, who worked with her husband Erskine Caldwell on a phototography book that documents the plight of Southern farmers during the Great Depression, titled You Have Seen Their Faces.
Life magazine's most iconic photograph may be Alfred Eisenstaedt's photo of a sailor and a nurse embracing following an announcement on August 14, 1945, which is known as this day.
This woman used her connections at Life to help fund Edward Steichen's The Family of Man exhibit. While working for the Farm Security Administration, she took the photograph Migrant Mother.

She highlighted the effects of the Great Depression on the poor in such photographs as Waiting for Relief Checks, taken in Calipatria, and Migrant Mother.
This man is best known for his Pulitzer-winning photograph of Mary Ann Vecchio screaming out at the shooting of Jeffrey Miller, taken while he was a student at Kent State.
